description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels. The block is solid and has a dark, almost black base, while the grooved top surface is a lighter, mottled gray-green, suggesting a granite or similar stone. The sides are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing the contrast with the textured top. The overall form is geometric and precise, indicating it may serve a functional purpose such as drainage or as a tactile paving element. Its robust construction and material suggest it is designed for outdoor urban environments where durability is essential.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, featuring a dark base and a lighter, mottled stone top. The grooved pattern suggests a functional urban use, such as drainage or tactile paving.
description_view_0: Front view showing the horizontal grooves running across the top surface, with the dark base visible below.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the vertical grooves, emphasizing the striped pattern.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the horizontal grooves and the solid, dark base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the vertical grooves and the alternating raised and recessed stone pattern.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or street drainage area
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with sharp groove edges.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: set flush with adjacent pavement or stone blocks
